title: Cannot stop machine stuck starting up
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I cannot stop a machine that is stuck starting.

Running `podman machine stop` reports success, but then `podman machine ls` still shows it is stuck starting.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Reboot my PC
2. Podman machine gets stuck starting for some reason
3. `docker machine ls` shows a machine `Currently starting`.
4. `docker machine stop` reports success stopping the machine.
5. `docker machine ls` still shows a machine `Currently starting`.

**Describe the results you received:**

The machine does not stop, despite the console log indicating it has.

**Describe the results you expected:**

The machine stops, or an error message is displayed indicating why the machine couldn't be stopped.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Error: failed to connect: dial tcp [::1]:59759: connect: connection refused
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
Error: failed to connect: dial tcp [::1]:59759: connect: connection refused
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

(brew)
```
==> podman: stable 4.3.1 (bottled), HEAD
Tool for managing OCI containers and pods
https://podman.io/
Not installed
From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/podman.rb
License: Apache-2.0 and GPL-3.0-or-later
==> Dependencies
Build: go-md2man ✘, go@1.18 ✘
Required: qemu ✘
==> Options
--HEAD
        Install HEAD version
==> Caveats
To restart podman after an upgrade:
  brew services restart podman
Or, if you don't want/need a background service you can just run:
  /usr/local/opt/podman/bin/podman system service --time=0
==> Analytics
install: 26,952 (30 days), 76,459 (90 days), 241,167 (365 days)
install-on-request: 24,997 (30 days), 72,020 (90 days), 235,361 (365 days)
build-error: 8 (30 days)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**

4.3.1 is the latest version on brew. This problem isn't in the troubleshooting guide.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

MacOS Ventura 13.0.1

comments.body: Does `podman --log-level=debug machine start` work? If not, what's are the log outputs of that? This might be a concurrency issue, so if there are any details on how to reproduce, that would be helpful.

Apologies, I needed to get my work done and what resolved it was removing the machine and recreating it. Sorry that this isn't very helpful on my part... Feel free to close if this isn't enough to go by.

If the issue happens again I will be sure to run that command and provide the output.
Sounds good, thanks @OscarVanL! Feel free to re-open if it happens again.

I was able to reproduce this pretty easily on MacOS Monterey 12.0.1. When running podman machine start it causes an OS level crash when attempting to start. I've heard this is because of a bug in MacOS that has since been fixed causing VM's to fail to launch, regardless after crashing the machine is stuck in limbo as indicated above.
Ran into the same issue m1 mac os Ventura 13.4

I encountered this issue today.
M1 max macOS Ventura 13.3.1 (a)
Podman 4.5.0 (installed through brew if that matters)

`podman machine stop` -> told was successful
`podman machine start` -> complaint that machine is already running or starting
`podman machine info` -> machine is perpetually starting; subsequent stops claim success but change nothing

The above persisted through a laptop restart.
I was forced to use `podman machine rm` to remove the machine. I then created a new one.

This bug still exists. MacOS Ventura 13.4, Podman 4.5.1, Podman Desktop 1.0.1.

Steps to reproduce: 
- Start Podman Desktop
- Stop Podman Desktop before Podman machine starts
- Podman machine ends up with infinity 'starting' state without possibility to stop it or actually start.

Only way to handle that is to remove Podman machine and recreate.

I am unsubscribing from the issue. 

I suggest those having similar issues to create your own issues. @ashley-cui suggested I should re-open the issue if it reoccurs, but as I no longer use podman so don't feel it would be appropriate for me to reopen it. At the same time I wouldn't want other people's experiences to go unheard because of my lack of activity :)
